title: Nannagaru
meta_description: Sri Nannagaru (1934–2017), born Bhupathiraju Venkata Lakshmi Narasimharaju, was a Telugu sage and devoted follower of Ramana Maharshi whose silent presence transmitted profound inner stillness to thousands.
body_html:
Life of Sri Nannagaru

Shri Bhupathiraju Venkata Lakshmi Narasimharaju (B.V.L.N. RAJU), as christened by his parents, was born on September 23, 1934 at his grandparent's village of Kommara in the West Godavari District of Andhra Pradesh. His parents, who were farmers, were devotional, kindly people. His father died when he was a child.

The young boy was gentle, humble and compassionate by nature. At twenty he went to North India on pilgrimage to the Himalayas and Varanasi, receiving blessings from Swami Sivananda of Rishikesh who told him to "be good and do good."

A significant event brought about a total change in Bhupathi's life. In 1957 he had a dream in which an old man with a staff raised him from the bed and kissed him hard on his cheeks. For six months he struggled to know who this stranger could be. He then received a book The Great Men of India by post, and recognised from the portrait the person from his dream: Sri Ramana Maharshi.

He made his first visit to Sri Ramanasramam in January, 1959, and from then on dedicated his life to the service of Sri Bhagavan. During a visit to Arunachala in 1982, while lying down in an Ashram Guest Room, Sri Bhagavan blessed him with the awakening of the Amruta Nadi and an experience of Bliss that continued thereafter.

He started to speak on Sri Bhagavan, gathering thousands of new devotees who had never heard of Arunachala or Sri Ramana Maharshi. He became endearingly known as Nannagaru — 'Nanna' in Telugu meaning 'Father' and 'Garu' being an honorific.

In 1984 Sri Nannagaru constructed at Jinnuru the 'Ramana Kshetram' dedicated to spreading Sri Ramana's direct teaching. He also built two ashrams at Tiruvannamalai, started a monthly Telugu magazine Ramana Bhaskara, and a Telugu-English website.

Narrative: My Experience with Sri Nannagaru

I first came across Sri Nannagaru in Ramanashram at Tiruvannamalai, in the last days of 1993. I saw a crowd of westerners sitting in silence before a man in his early 60s who was sitting on a chair. His eyes were drifting slowly from one person to another. I sat down. In moments I was aware of the depth of the silence. My body became as still as a stone. All thoughts vanished. His look was one of the most tender compassion, of a lover to his beloved; yet there was no sense of anybody doing anything, wanting anything. After half an hour or so, he silently bowed, and got up to go.

Sri Nannagaru at meeting with Devotees

Nannagaru lighting Deepam to Bhagavan Ramana

By Roger Adams

Sri Nannagaru Mahasamadhi

Sri Nannagaru dropped his mortal frame on December 29th, 2017 at 12:00 pm at his native place of Jinnuru, Andhra Pradesh.

Sri Nannagaru Samadhi
notes: Subtitle is dates. Long page with interview. Interview condensed; bio and narrative preserved.
